Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Verb phrase (transitive verb + noun); fixed idiomatic clause
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To remain patient and wait calmly for the right moment
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Vingila malembe sambu ntangu ya mbote kuma
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Common Kongo wisdom emphasizing patience in community life
First Example Sentence: Tata tubaka na mwana nde: dia ntangu, mambu nyonso ke kuma na ntangu na yo.
Second Example Sentence: Mono ke dia ntangu sambu na mvutu ya kisalu mono lombaka.
Third Example Sentence: Bakala ya mayele ke dia ntangu ntete yandi sala lukanu.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Verb phrase (verb + noun); metaphorical emotional construction
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To be courageous or emotionally strong in hardship
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Vanda na kikesa mpe landa malembe
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Rooted in Kongo cultural emphasis on inner strength

Third Example Sentence: Yandi simbisaka ntima ntangu makambu ya kisalu kumaka mpasi.

Second Example Sentence: Direktere kanga meso na mambu yina sambu na kimia.

Second Example Sentence: Bakento yai ke zenga nzila na mambu ya bizinesi.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Kongo moral philosophy about community empathy
